title: angophora costata subsp. euryphylla
intro: Angophora costata subsp. euryphylla, also known as the smooth-barked apple, is a subspecies of the Angophora costata tree native to eastern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Angophora costata subsp. euryphylla include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
